plain_description:
Building Embodied AI is a hands-on, rigorous guide to building AI systems that perceive, decide, act, and learn in the physical or simulated world.

The book develops embodied intelligence as a full stack: agent-environment interfaces, spatial representation, kinematics, dynamics, control, sensors, simulation, reinforcement learning, imitation learning, robot data, perception, language grounding, vision-language-action models, world models, manipulation, locomotion, humanoids, multi-agent systems, human-robot interaction, safety, robustness, deployment, memory, continual learning, and capstone course design.

Each chapter connects theory to implementation. Readers move from first-principles models to runnable recipes, worked examples, tool choices, engineering tradeoffs, failure modes, exercises, and labs. The emphasis is practical without becoming shallow: the book explains the mathematics and assumptions behind the methods, then shows how to build with modern libraries and systems such as Gymnasium, PettingZoo, MuJoCo, MJX, Isaac Lab, Genesis, Drake, ROS 2, PyTorch, JAX, simulation benchmarks, robot datasets, and deployment tooling.

This volume is part of the Hands-On AI Science series, a collection of self-contained books designed for serious self-study, university courses, research seminars, and engineering teams that need both conceptual depth and build fluency.

Readers should have basic Python and machine learning background. The book supplies the robotics, control, simulation, and embodied learning foundations needed to continue.
series_html_description:
Hands-On AI Science is a series of deep, build-it-yourself guides to the major fields of artificial intelligence.

Each book pairs serious scientific depth with practical building. The series develops each field from first principles to the current research frontier, then turns the ideas into runnable implementations, modern library workflows, engineering recipes, exercises, and labs.

The goal is to help readers understand both why AI methods work and how to build systems with them. Core ideas appear first through clear explanations, mental models, worked examples, and from-scratch implementations, then again through the tools and libraries used in real projects.

Each volume is self-contained and course-ready for advanced university study, research seminars, engineering teams, and serious self-study.

Books in the series include:

  • Building Language AI: From Tokens to Agents
  • Building Vision AI: From Pixels to Generative Models
  • Building Temporal AI: From Forecasting to Sequential Decision Making
  • Building Scalable AI: From Big Data Algorithms to Distributed Intelligence
  • Building Embodied AI: From Perception to Autonomous Action
short_description: A hands-on, rigorous guide to embodied AI, covering robotics foundations, simulation, reinforcement learning, robot data, perception, VLA models, world models, manipulation, locomotion, safety, deployment, and course-ready labs.

pricing.reason: The KPF is about 62.4 MiB, which can make 70 percent royalty delivery costs high. Verify the final delivery cost inside the KDP pricing page.
